titlestmt.titleproper: The Charles Henry Kauffman Collection of Materials Relating to Charles Ives
Kauffman (Charles Henry) Collection of Materials Relating to Charles Ives

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to <title xmlns:ns2="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" render="italic" ns2:type="simple">Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS)</title>.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday March 27, 2019 at 00:00
Restored Administrative Information section. Removed double name and address on title page.

did.unittitle: The Charles Henry Kauffman Collection of Materials Relating to Charles Ives
did.abstract: Chiefly materials pertaining to Charles Ives, from his friend Charles Henry Kauffman

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired in 2000 from Ralph Braibanti, the son-in-law of Charles Henry Kauffman.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open to researchers by appointment. There are no restricted materials in the collection. Please contact the Special Collections staff to schedule an appointment.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The The Charles Henry Kauffman Collection of Materials Relating to Charles Ives is the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Misc. Ms. 427, The Charles Henry Kauffman Collection of Materials Relating to Charles Ives in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Born in Pennsylvania, Dr. Charles Henry Kauffman practiced osteopathic surgery in Brookfield, Connecticut. Kauffman was a close friend of Charles Ives. The two first met in the 1920s through Ives's brother, Judge J. Moss Ives, and his brother-in-law, the Rev. Joseph Twichell. Dr. Kauffman treated both Charles Ives and his mother for various illnesses, and remained in contact with Ives throughout their lives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Much of the collection pertains to the composer Charles Ives. It includes letters between Kauffman and Ives (as well as other correspondents), published writings and music by Ives, articles concerning Ives, poetry by Kauffman, and a commemorative coin issued in honor of Ives.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into 5 series: I. Correspondence. II. Writings and Music. III. Clippings. IV. Programs. V. Miscellaneous.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Postcard from Paris. Date from postmark.

odd.head: General
odd.p: With typed transcription. Date supplied by Kauffman?

odd.head: General
odd.p: To President Franklin Roosevelt. Copy of Ives's memoranda concerning the Ludlow bill. Also includes his suggestion for a 20th amendment to the Constitution.

did.unittitle: Essays Before a Sonata. -- New York : Knickerbocker Press.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Inscribed "to Dr. Charles Kauffman from Charles E. Ives. Sep. 27, '25."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Note on flyleaf from Kauffman to Lex and Claire, Sept. 15, 1967.

odd.head: General
odd.p: "This article was accepted by New Yorker mag., but Ives refused permission to allow publication. Told to me by Ives' nephew Bigelow Ives. Sept. 30, 1965."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes a copy of his proposed 20th amendment to the Constitution.

did.unittitle: Notes concerning Ives's purchase of property in Brookfield.
Selected Poems of Charles Henry Kauffman. Compiled and introduced by Lucy Kauffman Braibanti and Ralph Braibanti.
Charles Edward Ives. -- New York : James T. White & Company.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Offprint from The National Cyclopedia of American Biography.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Yale alumni magazine, with cover story on Charles Ives.

persname.part: Yale University. School of Music. Oral History, American Music

odd.head: General
odd.p: Report concerning the Ives oral history project.
